pages.text: Spotty the Hyena is very sad. He has lost his laugh.
“Please help me find my laugh, Giraffe.” a r h e n ’ t c a “ I a l a u g h e. ” e r p h u
“Please help me find my laugh, Hippo.” “I can’t hear a laugh down here.”
“Please help me find my laugh, Warthog.” “ I c a n ’ t h e a r a e r e. ” i n h u g h l a
“Please help me find my laugh, Monkey.” “How did you lose it?”
“When I laugh, you can see my big teeth. That makes everyone frightened,” said Spotty. “Then I got sad and my laugh just disappeared.”
“ I c a n ’ t f i n d i t r e! ” w h e a n y “But you were looking in the wrong place.”
n k e y M o Then she began to tickle Spotty all over. j u m p e d tr e e f t h e o u t o and picked up a feather.
Slowly Spotty started smiling, and then he let out a big, loud laugh. He laughed and laughed until he was rolling around on the ground.
All the other animals started laughing too.
“Where did you find his laugh?” they asked. “His laugh was inside him all the time. I just made him happy and out it came.”
They all laughed and laughed so that their teeth showed too. o s e eve r l ’ ll n “ I m y l a u g h a g a i n, ” t h e o tt y d S p s a i h a pp y H y e n a.
